description: CAA Auto insurance JSON output format configuration

special_notes.date_format: Most date fields use MM/DD/YYYY format, except application_info.effective_date which uses YYYY-MM-DD
special_notes.data_types: All amount fields are strings, not numbers. Arrays always return [] when empty, objects return {} when empty
special_notes.dynamic_keys: vehicles_information uses vehicle model names as keys, drivers_information uses driver full names (FirstName LastName) as keys
special_notes.field_priority: address and phone fields prioritize quote document, fallback to application_info if not found

address.description: Customer address information - extracted from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Priority: Quote first, then Application

address.extraction_logic: Extract street address from Quote PDF. If not found or null, extract from Application PDF. Convert to uppercase.

city.extraction_logic: Extract city name from Quote PDF. If not found or null, extract from Application PDF. Convert to uppercase.

province.extraction_logic: Extract province code from Quote PDF. If not found or null, extract from Application PDF. Use standard Canadian abbreviations.

postal_code.extraction_logic: Extract postal code from Quote PDF. If not found or null, extract from Application PDF. Format: 'M1A 1A1' or 'M1A1A1'.

phone.description: Customer phone number information. Priority: Quote first, then Application

type.extraction_logic: Extract phone type from Quote PDF. If not found or null, extract from Application PDF.

number.extraction_logic: Extract phone number from Quote PDF. If not found or null, extract from Application PDF. Format logic: if length = 10 digits: 'XXX-XXX-XXXX'; if length > 10 digits: 'XXX-XXX-XXXX xXXXX' (where XXXX is the extension digits).
number.description: Phone number. Format: 'XXX-XXX-XXXX' for 10 digits, or 'XXX-XXX-XXXX xXXXX' for numbers with extension (where XXXX is the extension digits)

policy_information.description: Policy basic information - extracted from Policy Information section of Quote PDF

property_policy_type.extraction_logic: Extract property policy type from Quote PDF Policy Information section. Examples: 'Combined Policy / Multi-Line', 'Homeowners'.

property_carrier.extraction_logic: Extract whether property insurance exists from Quote PDF. Default to 'No' if not found.

property_policy_number.extraction_logic: Extract property policy number from Quote PDF.

property_policy_date.extraction_logic: Extract property policy date or term from Quote PDF. Examples: '12 Months'.
property_policy_date.description: Property policy date or term (e.g., '12 Months')

occupied_since.extraction_logic: Extract occupied since information from Quote PDF. Examples: '5 Years'.

policy_discount.extraction_logic: Extract policy discount type from Quote PDF.

all_carriers.extraction_logic: Extract all carriers information from Quote PDF. Examples: 'All Carriers'.

company_name.extraction_logic: Extract company name from Quote PDF.

term_length.extraction_logic: Extract term length from Quote PDF.

applicant_information.description: Applicant (primary insured) information. All name fields are automatically converted to uppercase.

salutation.extraction_logic: Extract salutation from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. Examples: 'MR', 'MRS', 'MS', 'DR'.

first_name.extraction_logic: Extract applicant's first name from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. This is a required field.
first_name.description: First name (required, automatically converted to uppercase)

middle_name.extraction_logic: Extract applicant's middle name from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase.

last_name.extraction_logic: Extract applicant's last name from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. This is a required field.
last_name.description: Last name (required, automatically converted to uppercase)

suffix.extraction_logic: Extract suffix from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. Examples: 'JR', 'SR', 'III'.

coapplicant_information.description: Co-applicant (co-insured) information. This object always exists. If no co-applicant, all fields are null but object is not null.

salutation.extraction_logic: Extract co-applicant's salutation from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. If no co-applicant found, return null.

first_name.extraction_logic: Extract co-applicant's first name from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. If no co-applicant found, return null. If First Name/Last Name labels are unclear or missing, use cross-document consistency: compare repeated full-name occurrences and align with driver_list/drivers_information naming (driver keys use 'FIRST LAST'). Keep this person's first_name/last_name order consistent across sections.

middle_name.extraction_logic: Extract co-applicant's middle name from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. If no co-applicant found, return null.

last_name.extraction_logic: Extract co-applicant's last name from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. If no co-applicant found, return null. If First Name/Last Name labels are unclear or missing, use cross-document consistency: compare repeated full-name occurrences and align with driver_list/drivers_information naming (driver keys use 'FIRST LAST'). Keep this person's first_name/last_name order consistent across sections.

suffix.extraction_logic: Extract co-applicant's suffix from Quote PDF or Application PDF. Convert to uppercase. If no co-applicant found, return null.

vehicle_list.description: Vehicle list - array of vehicle model names in order. Always exists, returns empty array [] if no vehicles.
vehicle_list.extraction_logic: Extract ALL vehicle model names from Quote PDF in order. Create an array of strings. Each string is a complete vehicle model name (e.g., '2019 NISSAN KICKS S 4DR 2WD'). Always return an array, even if empty.

vehicles_information.description: Vehicle detailed information - object with vehicle model names as keys. Each vehicle model name is a key, and the value contains all vehicle details.
vehicles_information.key_format: Vehicle model name (case-sensitive, must match exactly)
vehicles_information.extraction_logic: Extract ALL vehicles from Quote PDF. For each vehicle, use the complete vehicle model name as the key. Extract all vehicle details for that specific vehicle.

model_name.extraction_logic: Extract vehicle model name for THIS specific vehicle. This should match the key used for this vehicle in vehicles_information.
model_name.description: Vehicle model name (same as the key)

model_number.extraction_logic: Extract vehicle model number from parentheses for THIS specific vehicle. Example: '189800'.

vehicle_type.extraction_logic: Extract vehicle type for THIS specific vehicle. Examples: 'Private Passenger', 'Commercial'.

vin_number.extraction_logic: Extract VIN number for THIS specific vehicle. VIN should be 17 characters long.

body_style.extraction_logic: Extract body style for THIS specific vehicle. Examples: 'SUV - Small and Medium (Hard Top)', 'Sedan'.

fuel_type.extraction_logic: Extract fuel type for THIS specific vehicle.

hybrid.extraction_logic: Extract whether THIS specific vehicle is hybrid. Default to 'No' if not found.

primary_use.extraction_logic: Extract primary use for THIS specific vehicle. Examples: 'Pleasure', 'Business', 'Commute'.

annual_km.extraction_logic: Extract annual kilometers for THIS specific vehicle. Return as string.

business_km.extraction_logic: Extract business kilometers for THIS specific vehicle. IMPORTANT: business_km should ONLY have a value when primary_use is 'Business'. If primary_use is 'Pleasure', 'Commute', or 'Farm Use', business_km MUST be empty string ''. Do NOT confuse values from the adjacent 'Daily km' column with this field - read ONLY the cell directly under the 'Business km' header. Return as string.
business_km.description: Business kilometers (string type) - only applicable when primary_use is Business

daily_km.extraction_logic: Extract daily kilometers for THIS specific vehicle. Read ONLY the cell directly under the 'Daily km' header - do NOT confuse this with values from adjacent columns like 'Business km'. Small numbers (e.g. 8, 10) are normal for daily km; output them as strings. This field is independent of primary_use and can have a value regardless of what primary_use is set to. Return as string.

garaging_location.extraction_logic: Extract garaging location for THIS specific vehicle. Format: city and postal code, e.g., 'TORONTO M1A1A1'.

single_vehicle_MVD.extraction_logic: Extract single vehicle MVD status for THIS specific vehicle. Default to 'No' if not found.

leased.extraction_logic: Extract whether THIS specific vehicle is leased. Default to 'No' if not found.

cylinders.extraction_logic: Extract number of cylinders for THIS specific vehicle. Examples: '4', '6', '8'.

anti_theft_device_type.extraction_logic: Extract anti-theft device type for THIS specific vehicle.

anti_theft_manufacturer.extraction_logic: Extract anti-theft device manufacturer for THIS specific vehicle.

anti_theft_engraving.extraction_logic: Extract anti-theft device engraving for THIS specific vehicle.

purchase_condition.extraction_logic: Extract purchase condition for THIS specific vehicle. Examples: 'New', 'Used'.

purchase_date.extraction_logic: Extract purchase date for THIS specific vehicle. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.

km_at_purchase.extraction_logic: CRITICAL: Find the column with header 'km at Purchase'. Look DIRECTLY below that header. If the cell is BLANK/EMPTY, return null. If the cell has a value, use that value. DO NOT read left-to-right. DO NOT take values from 'List Price New' column even if 'km at Purchase' is blank. If 'km at Purchase' column is blank, km_at_purchase MUST be null.

list_price_new.extraction_logic: CRITICAL: Find the column with header 'List Price New'. Look DIRECTLY below that header. If the cell is BLANK/EMPTY, return null. If the cell has a value (like '29705'), use that value. DO NOT read left-to-right. DO NOT skip this column if 'km at Purchase' is blank - each column is independent. This is a PRICE field (digits like '29705', '25000').

purchase_price.extraction_logic: Extract ONLY the value that is visually aligned under the 'Purchase Price' column for THIS specific vehicle. Use strict table column alignment by header label. Do NOT shift values from adjacent columns. If the 'Purchase Price' cell is blank or unclear, return null.

winter_tires.extraction_logic: Extract winter tires status for THIS specific vehicle. Default to 'No' if not found.

parking_at_night.extraction_logic: Extract parking at night location for THIS specific vehicle. Examples: 'Private Garage', 'Street'.

drivers.extraction_logic: Extract drivers associated with THIS specific vehicle. Format: array of strings with salutation, full name, and relationship code. Example: ['MRS DAOYING GE (Prn)', 'MR JOHN DOE (Occ)']. Relationship codes: (Prn) = Principal, (Occ) = Occasional, (Sec) = Secondary, (Exc) = Excluded. Always return an array, even if empty [].
drivers.description: Drivers associated with this vehicle (array of strings with format: 'SALUTATION FIRSTNAME LASTNAME (RELATIONSHIP_CODE)')

driver_list.description: Driver list - array of driver full names in order. Always exists, returns empty array [] if no drivers.
driver_list.extraction_logic: Extract ALL driver full names from Quote PDF in order. Format: 'FirstName LastName' (space between first and last name, automatically converted to uppercase). Create an array of strings. Always return an array, even if empty.

drivers_information.description: Driver detailed information - object with driver full names as keys. Each driver full name (FirstName LastName) is a key, and the value contains all driver details.
drivers_information.key_format: Driver full name: 'FirstName LastName' (space between, automatically converted to uppercase, no salutation)
drivers_information.extraction_logic: Extract ALL drivers from Quote PDF, MVR PDFs, and Application PDF. For each driver, use 'FirstName LastName' (uppercase, no salutation) as the key. Extract all driver details for that specific driver.

first_name.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's first name. Convert to uppercase.

last_name.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's last name. Convert to uppercase.

date_of_birth.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's date of birth. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
date_of_birth.description: Date of birth in MM/DD/YYYY format

marital_status.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's marital status. Examples: 'Married', 'Single', 'Divorced'.

relationship_to_applicant.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's relationship to applicant. Examples: 'Applicant', 'Spouse', 'Child'.

driver_training.extraction_logic: Extract whether THIS specific driver has driver training. Default to 'No' if not found.

driver_training_date.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's driver training date. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
driver_training_date.description: Driver training date in MM/DD/YYYY format

out_of_province_country_driver.extraction_logic: Extract whether THIS specific driver is out of province/country driver. Default to 'No' if not found.

driver_licence_class.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's licence class. Examples: 'G', 'G2', 'G1', 'M'.

date_G.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's G class licence date. Format: MM/DD/YYYY. Only include if driver_licence_class is 'G'.
date_G.description: G class licence date in MM/DD/YYYY format

date_G2.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's G2 class licence date. Format: MM/DD/YYYY. Include if driver_licence_class is 'G2' or 'G'.
date_G2.description: G2 class licence date in MM/DD/YYYY format

date_G1.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's G1 class licence date. Format: MM/DD/YYYY. Include if driver_licence_class is 'G1', 'G2', or 'G'.
date_G1.description: G1 class licence date in MM/DD/YYYY format

driver_licence_number.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's licence number.

licence_province.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's licence province. Use standard Canadian abbreviations.

occupation.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's occupation. Examples: 'Retired', 'Engineer', 'Student'.

date_insured.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's date insured. Format: MM/DD/YYYY. IMPORTANT: If extracted value does not match MM/DD/YYYY format (validated by string_is_m_d_y_format function), return null.
date_insured.description: Date insured in MM/DD/YYYY format. Returns null if format validation fails.

current_carrier.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's current insurance carrier.

date_with_company.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's date with company. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
date_with_company.description: Date with company in MM/DD/YYYY format

brokerage_insured.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's brokerage insured date. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
brokerage_insured.description: Brokerage insured date in MM/DD/YYYY format

current_Policy #.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's current policy number from Autoplus. In Autoplus, go to the 'Policies' section and find the column labeled 'Policy #' (or the 'policy' column). Take the FIRST insurance entry's Policy # (the topmost non-empty Policy # value in that column). Return the policy number as a string only (remove surrounding whitespace; remove spaces inside the number if present, but keep letters/digits/hyphens). If Autoplus/dash is missing (not provided) or there is no Policies content for this driver, treat as no insurance record and return null. Do NOT infer this value from Quote/Application text when Autoplus is missing.
current_Policy #.description: Current policy number (from Autoplus first policy entry)

date_owner_principal.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's date owner/principal. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.

applicant_lives_with_parents.extraction_logic: Extract whether applicant lives with parents for THIS specific driver. Default to 'No' if not found.

student_away_at_school.extraction_logic: Extract student away at school information for THIS specific driver.

retired.extraction_logic: Extract whether THIS specific driver is retired. Default to 'No' if not found.

other_licence_class.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's other licence class.

other_licence_date.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's other licence date. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
other_licence_date.description: Other licence date in MM/DD/YYYY format

claims.extraction_logic: Extract ALL claims for THIS specific driver from Quote PDF and AP Quote PDFs. Merge claims from all sources. Each claim is an object. Always return an array, even if empty []. All amount fields (tp/bi, tp/pd$, ab$, coll$, other_pd$) are strings, not numbers. IMPORTANT: policy field (Claim# / Policy#) is REQUIRED when a claim exists and must NOT be empty.
claims.description: Claims array - always exists, returns empty array [] if no claims

description.description: Claim description (e.g., 'Non-resp Direct Compensation', 'At Fault Collision')

vehicle_involved.description: Vehicle model name involved in claim

policy.description: Claim# / Policy# for this claim. Must be extracted when claim exists and must not be empty.

tp/bi.description: Third party bodily injury amount (string type)

tp/pd$.description: Third party property damage amount (string type, e.g., '5000')

convictions.extraction_logic: Extract ALL convictions for THIS specific driver from Quote PDF and MVR PDFs. Merge convictions from all sources. Each conviction is an object. Always return an array, even if empty [].
convictions.description: Convictions array - always exists, returns empty array [] if no convictions

suspensions.extraction_logic: Extract ALL suspensions for THIS specific driver. Each suspension is an object. Always return an array, even if empty [].
suspensions.description: Suspensions array - always exists, returns empty array [] if no suspensions

lapses.extraction_logic: Extract ALL lapses for THIS specific driver. Each lapse is an object. Always return an array, even if empty [].
lapses.description: Lapses array - always exists, returns empty array [] if no lapses

description.description: Lapse description (e.g., 'No Vehicle Hence No Insurance Required')

vehicles.extraction_logic: This field is automatically populated by mapDriversToVehicles function based on vehicles_information.drivers fields. Contains array of vehicle model names associated with THIS specific driver. Vehicle model names must exactly match keys in vehicles_information (case-sensitive). Always return an array, even if empty [].
vehicles.description: Vehicles associated with this driver (array of vehicle model name strings)

carrier_information.description: Carrier information - extracted from Carrier section of Quote PDF. If multiple carriers found, extract only the first one. If no carrier found, return null.

carrier_name.extraction_logic: Extract carrier name from Quote PDF Carrier section. If multiple carriers found, extract only the first one. Examples: 'CNA Insurance COMPANY', 'Intact Insurance'.
carrier_name.description: Carrier name (string type, e.g., 'CNA Insurance COMPANY', 'Intact Insurance')

annual_premium.extraction_logic: Extract annual premium from Quote PDF Carrier section. Format as string: '$X,XXX.XX'. Example: '$1,558.00'.

total_premium.extraction_logic: Extract total premium from Quote PDF Carrier section. Format as string: '$X,XXX.XX'. Example: '$1,558.00'.

discounts_information.description: Discount information - grouped by vehicle. Object with vehicle model names as keys. If no discounts found, return empty object {}.
discounts_information.key_format: Vehicle model name (must exactly match vehicles_information keys, case-sensitive)
discounts_information.extraction_logic: Extract discount information from Quote PDF, grouped by vehicle. For each vehicle, use vehicle model name as key. Discount structure has two variants: if discount text contains '=' symbol, include both percentage and driver_covered; if no '=' symbol, include only driver_covered.

DISCOUNT_TYPE.extraction_logic: Extract discount type as key. Value is object with percentage (if '=' found in text) and/or driver_covered. Examples: '25 • Years Claims Free = 5%', 'Multi-Line = 10%'.

driver_covered.description: Driver covered (e.g., 'Prn' for Principal, 'Occ' for Occasional)

variant_2.condition: If discount text does not contain '=' symbol

driver_covered.description: Driver covered (only driver info, no percentage)

coverages_information.description: Coverage information - grouped by vehicle. Object with vehicle model names as keys. If no coverages found, return empty object {}. All amount fields (principal, totals, coverage_amount) are strings, not numbers. IMPORTANT: This section includes BOTH standard coverages AND OPCF options.
coverages_information.key_format: Vehicle model name (must exactly match vehicles_information keys, case-sensitive)
coverages_information.extraction_logic: Extract ALL coverage information from Quote PDF, grouped by vehicle. This includes: (1) Standard coverages like 'Bodily Injury', 'Property Damage', 'All Perils', 'Accident Benefits', 'Direct Compensation', 'Uninsured Automobile'; (2) OPCF options and additional protections like '#5 Rent or Lease', '#20 Loss of Use', '#27 Liab to Unowned Veh.', '#43a Limited Waiver', '#44 Family Protection', 'Minor Conviction Protection', 'Forgive & Forget', etc. CRITICAL: Look for ALL rows in coverage/premium tables, including those with operator protection types. Extract EVERY coverage and protection option you see in the Quote PDF - do not skip any! For each vehicle, use vehicle model name as key. Coverage type or OPCF option name is key, value contains principal, totals, and/or coverage_amount. All three fields may exist, or some may be null, depending on coverage type and extraction result.

COVERAGE_TYPE.extraction_logic: Extract coverage type or OPCF option name as key. Examples of standard coverages: 'Bodily Injury', 'Property Damage', 'All Perils', 'Accident Benefits', 'Direct Compensation', 'Uninsured Automobile'. Examples of OPCF options and protections: '#5 Rent or Lease', '#20 Loss of Use', '#27 Liab to Unowned Veh.', '#43a Limited Waiver', '#44 Family Protection', 'Minor Conviction Protection', 'Forgive & Forget'. Value is object with principal, totals, and/or coverage_amount fields. CRITICAL CHECKLIST: When extracting, make sure you capture ALL rows from coverage/premium tables, including: (1) All standard coverages, (2) All OPCF options (those starting with #), (3) All protection options like 'Forgive & Forget', 'Minor Conviction Protection', etc. Do NOT skip any rows - extract EVERY coverage and protection option you see in the Quote PDF tables!
COVERAGE_TYPE.description: Coverage type or OPCF option name (e.g., 'Bodily Injury', 'Property Damage', 'All Perils', '#20 Loss of Use', '#27 Liab to Unowned Veh.', '#43a Limited Waiver', '#44 Family Protection', 'Minor Conviction Protection')

principal.extraction_logic: Extract principal coverage amount. Format: '1 M' (1 million), '1,000' (1 thousand), '50K' (50 thousand), etc. May be null if not found.
principal.description: Principal coverage amount (string type, e.g., '1 M', '1,000', '50K', may be null)

totals.extraction_logic: Extract total premium amount. Format: '181', '9', '383', etc. This field always exists.
totals.description: Total premium amount (string type, e.g., '181', '9', '383', always exists)

coverage_amount.extraction_logic: Extract formatted coverage amount. CRITICAL FORMAT REQUIREMENTS: (1) Do NOT include $ symbol (use '1,000,000' NOT '$1,000,000'), (2) Do NOT include text descriptions (use '1,000' NOT '$1,000 Ded.'), (3) Use '0' for zero deductible (NOT 'No Deductible' or '$0 Ded.'), (4) Use standard number format ('1,000,000') or K/M suffix ('1M', '50K'), (5) Preserve special values exactly ('Standard', 'Inc.', '60 Months'). Format examples: '1M', '1,000', '50K', '0', 'Standard', 'Inc.'. May be null if cannot be extracted from text.
coverage_amount.description: Formatted coverage amount (string type, e.g., '1M', '1,000', '50K', '0', 'Standard', 'Inc.', may be null). MUST NOT contain $ symbol or text descriptions like 'Ded.'

opcf_options.description: OPCF (Ontario Policy Change Form) options and additional coverages - extracted from Quote PDF. These are optional coverage options that appear in tables or lists in the quote document.
opcf_options.key_format: Vehicle model name (must exactly match vehicles_information keys, case-sensitive)
opcf_options.extraction_logic: Extract OPCF options from Quote PDF, grouped by vehicle. Look for tables or lists containing OPCF options like '#5 Rent or Lease', '#20 Loss of Use', '#27 Liab to Unowned Veh.', '#43a Limited Waiver (Months)', etc. For each vehicle, use vehicle model name as key. Each option should be an object with option_name, value, and premium fields.

OPTION_NAME.extraction_logic: Extract OPCF option name as key. Examples: 'Rent or Lease', 'Loss of Use', 'Liab to Unowned Veh.', 'Limited Waiver (Months)'. Value is object with value, principal, and totals fields.
OPTION_NAME.description: OPCF option name (e.g., 'Rent or Lease', 'Loss of Use', 'Liab to Unowned Veh.', 'Limited Waiver (Months)')

value.extraction_logic: Extract the value/amount for this OPCF option. Examples: 'Inc.' (for Included), '1,500', '50K', '60' (for months). Convert abbreviations: K = thousands (50K = 50000). May be null if not found.
value.description: Option value/amount (string type, e.g., 'Inc.', '1,500', '50K', '60', may be null)

principal.extraction_logic: Extract principal premium amount for this OPCF option. Format: '39', '31', '193', etc. May be null if not found.
principal.description: Principal premium amount (string type, e.g., '39', '31', '193', may be null)

totals.extraction_logic: Extract total premium amount for this OPCF option. Format: '39', '31', '193', etc. May be null if not found.
totals.description: Total premium amount (string type, e.g., '39', '31', '193', may be null)

application_info.description: Application form information - extracted from Application PDF

number.extraction_logic: Extract phone number from Application PDF. Format logic: if length = 10 digits: 'XXX-XXX-XXXX'; if length > 10 digits: 'XXX-XXX-XXXX xXXXX' (where XXXX is the extension digits).
number.description: Phone number. Format: 'XXX-XXX-XXXX' for 10 digits, or 'XXX-XXX-XXXX xXXXX' for numbers with extension

lienholders.description: Lienholders list - always exists, returns empty array [] if no lienholders
lienholders.extraction_logic: Extract lienholders from Application PDF. Each lienholder is an object. Always return an array, even if empty [].

address.description: Lienholder address (array type, contains address parts)

lessor.extraction_logic: Extract lessor information from Application PDF.

caa_membership.extraction_logic: Extract CAA membership status from Application PDF or Quote PDF. CRITICAL: Search for the EXACT pattern 'Group discount apply: yes - CAA' (with or without '| Member #:' following it). IMPORTANT: This pattern often appears in LATER sections of documents - search the ENTIRE document, especially the END sections of Application PDF and ALL sections of Quote PDF. The pattern may ONLY appear in Quote PDF, not in Application PDF. It typically appears in MEMO TEXT DETAILS section or similar memo/remarks/details sections (often near the end of documents). If you find 'Group discount apply: yes - CAA' anywhere in ANY document, you MUST set caa_membership to 'Yes'. Also check for patterns like 'CAA | Member #:' or 'CAA Member #:' - if a CAA membership number exists, set to 'Yes'. Examples of patterns to look for: 'Group discount apply: yes - CAA', 'Group discount apply: yes - CAA | Member #: 620 2822 4256 53003'. If this pattern is found, caa_membership MUST be 'Yes'. Only set to 'No' if you have thoroughly searched ALL documents (including later sections) and found NO indication of CAA membership.

caa_membership_number.extraction_logic: Extract CAA membership number from Application PDF or Quote PDF. CRITICAL: Look for the EXACT pattern 'Group discount apply: yes - CAA | Member #: [NUMBER]' where [NUMBER] is the membership number. IMPORTANT: This often appears in LATER sections of documents - search the ENTIRE document, especially the END sections of Application PDF and ALL sections of Quote PDF. The pattern may ONLY appear in Quote PDF. It typically appears in MEMO TEXT DETAILS section (often near the end of documents). The number may be formatted with spaces (e.g., '620 2822 4256 53003') or without spaces. Also search for patterns like 'Member #: [NUMBER]' when it appears near 'CAA' or 'Group discount apply: yes - CAA'. Extract the complete membership number. You may remove spaces for consistency (e.g., '6202822425653003'), but preserving spaces is also acceptable. CRITICAL RULE: If you find 'Group discount apply: yes - CAA' in ANY document (including later sections), you MUST also find and extract the membership number that follows it (usually on the same line after '| Member #:'). The number format is typically digits with optional spaces (e.g., '620 2822 4256 53003' or '6202822425653003'). If caa_membership is 'Yes', this field MUST be extracted and CANNOT be null or empty.
caa_membership_number.description: CAA membership number (string or null). MANDATORY when caa_membership is 'Yes' - must be extracted from documents.

effective_date.extraction_logic: Extract policy effective date from Application PDF. IMPORTANT: This is the ONLY date field that uses YYYY-MM-DD format. All other date fields use MM/DD/YYYY format.
effective_date.description: Policy effective date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2026-03-01') - NOTE: This is the ONLY field using this format

provider.extraction_logic: Extract previous insurance carrier name from Application PDF.

policy_number.extraction_logic: Extract previous policy number from Application PDF.

expiry_date.extraction_logic: Extract previous expiry date from Application PDF. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
expiry_date.description: Previous expiry date in MM/DD/YYYY format
